name: Controlled Radical Polymerization (ATRP/RAFT)
description: Controlled/living radical polymerization keeps a low instantaneous radical concentration through a fast dynamic equilibrium between active and dormant chains; when the equilibrium strongly favors the dormant species, all chains grow at the same slow rate, giving predictable molar mass and low dispersity once the target is reached.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Monomer + control agent"]
    N2["Activate dormant species"]
    N3["Active <=> dormant equilibrium"]
    N4{"Favors dormant?"}
    N5["Tune catalyst / CTA"]
    N6["Low instantaneous [radical]"]
    N7["Add monomers, re-cap"]
    N8["Uniform chain growth"]
    N9{"Target MW reached?"}
    N10["Low-dispersity polymer"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|no| N5
    N5 -->|retune| N2
    N4 -->|yes| N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 -->|more cycles| N7
    N9 -->|yes| N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Living free-radical polymerization by reversible addition-fragmentation chain transfer: the RAFT process

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
